sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 20.58 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.20)

statement.latex: Let$(G)$denote the set of element orders of a finite group$G$. A finite group~$G$is said to be recognizable (by spectrum) if every finite group$H$with$(H)= (G)$is isomorphic to$G$. [r]{ a)} Is it true that for every$n$there is a recognizable group that is the$n$-th direct power of a~nonabelian simple group? [r]{b)} Is it true that there is a nonabelian simple group$L$such that for every$n$there is...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory I_INFINITE_SEARCH; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
